title: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR): Obligations in Germany
bodyHtml:
Disclaimer: Note that Back Market doesn’t provide legal advice, and that it’s your responsibility to remain up to date with existing regulations. For additional information, contact your legal advisors.
 

What’s EPR? 

Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is an environmental policy that makes the party who first places an item on the market subject to EPR requirements. This party is also known as the “producer.” They’re responsible for the entire lifecycle of the item, from design to end-of-life disposal, including waste collection and treatment). Under EPR regulations, the producer must mitigate the environmental impacts of their items throughout the entire item lifecycle by paying a fee. 
 

How does the EPR system work in Germany? 

The German government has introduced a new version of the Electric and Electronic Equipment Act (ElektroG), the German Packaging Act (VerpackungsG), and the Batteries Act (BattG). 

Under this regulation, platforms such as Back Market are legally required to prevent and manage waste and make sure that its partners selling in the German market have an EPR registration number for Packaging (from July 2022), Batteries (starting July 1, 2023), and Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) (starting July 1, 2023). 

There are many different categories of EPR: Batteries, Packaging, Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), etc. Each of these categories have 1 or more Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) who’s responsible for registering the producers and providing them with EPR registration numbers and collecting fees.

Partners are responsible for registering with the relevant organizations. If not compliant, Back Market will have to suspend them from selling in the German market. 
 

Am I a producer under German regulations? 

A producer isn’t the same as a manufacturer. 

Packaging 

As a retailer, you’re considered a producer if you’re the first to place packages filled with goods on the German market on a commercial basis.

Regarding primary packaging (packaging for the item itself), if your supplier has already put the packaging on the German market, your supplier may be considered the producer and you may provide your supplier’s registration number. 

Regarding secondary packaging (shipment packaging), all sellers are considered producers and must register, report, and pay the fees. 

Check out this ZSVR infographic for more details


Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) and Batteries 

You’re considered a producer if:

  • You import an item subject to EPR requirements in Germany. 
  • You sell an item subject to EPR requirements in Germany but are not established in Germany (in this case, you may need to appoint a representative in Germany).
  • You manufacture an item subject to EPR requirements in Germany.

 


What items are subject to EPR? 

Several categories of items sold on Back Market fall under these requirements, including:

  • Packaging 
  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) 
  • Batteries 

With some exceptions, you must get a unique EPR registration number for each category.

For example, if you’re selling phones and tablets, you need to register for 3 different EPR categories: Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), Batteries, and Packaging. This means you’ll get 3 different EPR registration numbers. 

Here are the Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs) you need to register with for each category: 

EPR categoryProducer Responsibility Organization (PRO) to register with 
Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)https://www.stiftung-ear.de/en/home
Batterieshttps://www.stiftung-ear.de/en/home
Packaging
https://lucid.verpackungsregister.org/
 


Depending on the type of items you sell, you may need to contact other Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs) who handle other categories of items.
 

How do I register? 

Note that these steps apply only to Germany. You’ll need to register separately for other markets such as France or Austria. 

Packaging  

You’re responsible for both primary and secondary packaging, so here’s that you need to do to register for Packaging:

Register with the central packaging agency Zentrale Stelle Verpackungsregister-ZSVR through the Lucid Packaging Register. After you register, the ZSVR will provide you with your EPR registration number that begins with “DE” and is followed by 13 digits. 

You need to fulfill the system participation requirements, so you’ll have to enter into a system participation agreement with 1 or more authorized recycling systems (see here for a list).  By providing your Lucid registration number to the authorized recycling system of your choice, you’ll be able to get a dual membership certificate. 

Once you’ve gotten your Lucid registration number and the dual system certificate of membership, you must add them to the “Legal details” section of your Back Office. Keep reading for Back Market's collection process. 

If you need more information, see ZSVR's FAQs  


Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)  

From July 1, 2023, Back Market will be legally required to make sure that you comply with EPR obligations in the Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) category. You must register with the EAR Foundation, who will issue you a number.

If you don’t already have these registration numbers, we suggest you apply now as the process may take a few weeks. Register with the EAR Foundation now

Once you’ve gotten your registration number, add it to the “Legal details” section of your Back Office. Keep reading for Back Market's collection process. 

Need more information? See the Stiftung EAR website


Batteries

From July 1, 2023, Back Market will be legally required to make sure that you comply with EPR obligations in the Batteries category. You must register with the EAR Foundation, who will issue you a number.

If you don’t already have these registration numbers, we suggest you apply now as the process may take a few weeks. Register with the EAR Foundation now

Once you’ve gotten your registration number, add it to the “Legal details” section of your Back Office. Keep reading for Back Market's collection process. 

Need more information? See the Stiftung EAR website

If you need assistance, some Back Market partners offer support services: 

  • For Packaging, check out Reclay and get 5% off when you use promo code BACK5ACT
  • For Packaging, Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), and Batteries, check out Take-e-way 

Note that these providers are independent of Back Market. 
 

I don’t have a business entity in Germany. What do I need to do? 

If you don’t have a business entity established in Germany, you may be required to appoint an authorized representative to register on your behalf. Get more information here  
 

Is registration mandatory in Germany?

Yes. If you don’t comply with the EPR requirements for Packaging, Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), and Batteries, Back Market will have to suspend you from selling in the German market. 

Make sure you add valid EPR registration numbers for all categories in Germany in the “Legal details” section of your Back Office.
 

What do I need to do after registering? 

Once registered, you must : 

  • Add your EPR registration number for each category depending on the type of items you sell. 
  • Make sure your EPR registration numbers are publicly available on the official databases (Lucid for Packaging and the EAR Foundation for Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) and Batteries. 
  • Report the quantity of items that you sold in Germany. 
  • Pay fees to the Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs). 

The fees are calculated by each Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) according to sales, categories of items, and the volume of items put on the market. 
 

What numbers should I add to my Back Office? 

You’ll have to give Back Market your EPR number for each category. Please note that if you have several accounts you must fill in your EPR number for each account. 

Back Market will verify your numbers once submitted in your Back Office.

The format of each EPR number is as follows: 

  • Packaging: DEXXXXXXXXXXXXX (13 numbers)*
  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE): DEXXXXXXXX (8 numbers)
  • Batteries: DEXXXXXXXX (8 numbers)

*In addition to the number for the Packaging category, you’ll also need to add your dual system membership certificate in the “Legal details” section of your Back Office. 

See screenshot below:



Click “Add details.”



To upload your packaging certificate, choose “Packaging.” 



Finally, choose your category and add the corresponding EPR registration number. 
 

I’m buying items from an upstream supplier. What do I need to do? 

Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) and Batteries categories
 
If you’re buying items from an upstream supplier and you’re not producing or importing items that fall under these categories, you need to be sure that your upstream supplier is registered with the EAR Foundation. In such cases, you’ll need to add the EPR numbers of your supplier. 

For the Packaging category 

For the primary packaging, you can add your upstream supplier’s EPR number if your supplier is considered a producer. 

For the secondary packaging, you’ll always be the producer, so you need to have your own Packaging EPR registration number. 
 

What if my supplier doesn’t give me their EPR number? 

If your upstream supplier doesn’t want to give you their EPR registration number, you must register with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) and provide your EPR registration number to Back Market. Otherwise,  Back Market will suspend you from selling in the German market. 
 

I only sell refurbished items. Am I exempt from EPR requirements in Germany? 

If you buy second-hand products from Germany: you don't need your own number if you don't sell them under your own brand.
In this case, you do not need to submit a registration number, but you must certify under your own responsibility that you are exempt.

If you buy products outside Germany to sell on the German market, you are considered to be an importer and must therefore obtain a WEEE registration number and send it to Back Market.

 

Where can I find more information about EPR obligations in Germany? 

If you want information about a particular EPR category, you can check the Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) websites (see above for details). 

For Packaging, check out ZSVR’s FAQs here

For Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) and Batteries, check out Stiftung EAR’s FAQs here  

We know EPR requirements are complicated. Back Market has a network of compliance partners who can help.

Reach out to Reclay and get 5% off when you use promo code BACK5ACT

Or check out Take e-way to learn more
